commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] There is one narrow, actionable blocker: make uncacheable auth state non-cacheable for persisted model catalog cache callers and add regression coverage.; Needs attention: A concrete auth-provider cache issue remains: uncacheable auth state can become a reusable persisted catalog cache key.; Review finding: [P1] Do not reuse catalog cache keys for uncacheable auth state â€” src/agents/models-config.ts:1857 (sha=2b3d939fd471110d70e5f9437a0d02bce43f874f)
commands.review_summary:
The PR unifies models-config content fingerprinting with provider-scoped targetProvider/targetModel short-circuiting, adds canonical SQLite auth-store reads, validates plugin catalog sidecars, wires embedded callers, and adds regression/proof scripts.

PR surface: Source +1550, Tests +2032, Other +744. Total +4326 across 23 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: the PR returns a stable uncacheable auth fingerprint and current callers use that fingerprint to read the persisted catalog cache before forcing models-config preparation. I did not run a mutating repro because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] There is one narrow, actionable blocker: make uncacheable auth state non-cacheable for persisted model catalog cache callers and add regression coverage.

target.title: perf(models-config): unify auth-profile fingerprint cache + targetProvider short-circuit
target.body:
## Summary

Supersedes #73260 and #73261 with one reconciled implementation. The two are **complementary, not redundant** â€” both avoid the expensive `resolveImplicitProviders` path (plugin discovery fan-out + live provider-catalog fetches), layered in `ensureOpenClawModelsJson` as: in-process whole-input **fingerprint cache** (warm-process memoizer) â†’ provider-scoped **structural short-circuit** (cold-start / cross-provider-change skip) â†’ full plan.

Reconciled here:

- **#73260** â€” content-hash auth-profile fingerprint + `models.json` drift detection. Warm-process whole-input memoizer; OAuth token rotation no longer busts the cache.
- **#73261** â€” provider-scoped `targetProvider` structural short-circuit with disk-vs-config validation. Skips discovery on cold start / gateway restart, or when an *unrelated* provider changed.

Plus a correctness fix shared by both PRs, and carried refinements:

- **Fingerprint the canonical SQLite auth store** (`openclaw-agent.sqlite`, via the warm pooled handle) instead of the legacy `auth-profiles.json` file. Both prior PRs hashed the legacy JSON, which reads `absent` for every agent already migrated to SQLite â€” so real credential changes never invalidated the implicit-provider-discovery cache. Reading the canonical store fixes that, removes the symlink / oversize-file attack surface (it's a DB read, not a file read), and aligns with the "SQLite only / no legacy runtime readers" storage policy. Volatile OAuth session fields are still stripped before hashing, so token rotation stays cache-stable.
- **Per-type token-expiry volatile fields** â€” `type:"token"` profiles keep `expires*` in the hash so an expired static token invalidates the cache, while OAuth session rotation does not.
- **Plugin-catalog wiring** â€” merge generated plugin catalogs into the pre-plan parsed `models.json` and write them in the skip / noop / write paths.

All security hardenings from both PRs are preserved (fail-closed `uncacheable`, prototype-pollution guards, bounded compare depth, per-model transport refusal, validated-outcome TOCTOU close, scoped-cache isolation).

## Verification

- `pnpm tsgo:core` â†’ clean
- `pnpm tsgo:core:test` â†’ clean
- `pnpm vitest run` (the 3 acceptance-criteria files: `models-config.fingerprint-cache.test.ts`, `models-config.write-serialization.test.ts`, `models-config.target-provider-short-circuit.test.ts`) â†’ **112 passed**, including 4 new regression tests pinning sidecar drift + no-DB-create
- `pnpm config:schema:check` â†’ ok
- `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check` â†’ ok
- `pnpm oxlint` (touched files) â†’ clean of new errors (2 pre-existing `no-promise-executor-return` lines are outside this diff)
- `git diff --check` â†’ clean

## Real behavior proof

Real-runtime proof script `scripts/proof-90741-plugin-catalog-cache-validation.ts` drives the real `ensureOpenClawModelsJson` against a real on-disk agent directory, real generated plugin-catalog sidecars, the real `readyCache` populate/drift cycle, and the real no-create auth-store read. **No mocking of the seam under test**; only the network edge is avoided (static `apiKey`, no live provider catalog fetch).

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** A plugin-catalog sidecar deleted or tampered after a warm models-config cache entry was created still hit the cache and skipped reconciliation (provider-routing integrity hole, [P1]); separately, the read-only auth fingerprint computation created the agent SQLite DB as a write side effect ([P2]). After the fix, sidecar drift busts the warm cache and the rogue file is reconciled away, and the fingerprint read never materializes the auth DB.
- **Real environment tested:** Real `ensureOpenClawModelsJson` runtime path driven against an on-disk temp agent directory with real generated plugin-catalog sidecars, the real `readyCache` populate/drift cycle, and the real no-create auth-store read. Topic worktree at head `e1524762ec`. No mocking of the seam under test; only the network edge is avoided (static apiKey, no live provider fetch).
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `node --import tsx scripts/proof-90741-plugin-catalog-cache-validation.ts` (equivalently `pnpm tsx scripts/proof-90741-plugin-catalog-cache-validation.ts`), run from the topic worktree.
- **Evidence after fix:** Captured terminal output (stdout) from the live run of the proof against the real cache code path:

```
[1/3] sidecar-drift-busts-cache-and-reconciles
    rogue sidecar present after re-plan: false
    ok
[2/3] fingerprint-read-does-not-create-agent-db
    auth DB created: false, -wal: false, -shm: false
    ok
[3/3] stable-no-sidecar-still-hits
    models.json mtimeMs cold=1780794326032.3853 warm=1780794326032.3853 ino cold=3879005 warm=3879005
    ok

All runtime assertions passed.
```

- **Observed result after fix:** All three runtime assertions passed (process exit 0). Scenario 1 â€” the rogue sidecar is absent after re-plan (`rogue sidecar present after re-plan: false`): the warm cache correctly missed on sidecar drift and `removeStalePluginCatalogs` reconciled the tampered file away. Scenario 2 â€” no `openclaw-agent.sqlite` / `-wal` / `-shm` was created by the fingerprint read (`auth DB created: false, -wal: false, -shm: false`): the read-only cache-key computation no longer has a write side effect. Scenario 3 â€” steady-state no-sidecar inputs still hit the cache (identical `models.json` inode and mtime cold vs warm), confirming no perf regression.
- **What was not tested:** A fully live multi-provider gateway boot is not exercised; the cache populate/drift/reconcile and no-create auth-read paths are driven end-to-end against real on-disk state by the proof script above. Unit coverage (`models-config.fingerprint-cache.test.ts` + 3 acceptance files, 112 passed) is supplemental.
target.author: zeroaltitude

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
